metadata_checks.comments: Title, DOI, year, and paper type match. Scope is correct: municipal support/opposition to Swiss taxes on fossil/non-renewable energy, including one with revenue recycling.

determinant_checks.comment: Direct ideological determinant; strongly positive and significant across the tax votes.
Direct social-norms determinant; positive and significant across the tax votes.
Demographic control; not a core substantive tax determinant.
Demographic control; not a core substantive tax determinant.
Socioeconomic determinant; positive and significant across the tax votes.
Direct cost-exposure determinant; negative and significant across the tax votes.
Core economic determinant; nonlinear inverted-U relationship with support.
Demographic control; effect weakens in the full model.
Contextual control; sign varies by specification.
Contextual control; effect disappears once ideology is included.
Regional control; not a core policy determinant.
Direct exposure measure; negative overall, especially for the green tax reform.
Direct exposure measure; negative and significant for solar and green tax votes.
Direct exposure measure; negative in both specifications.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: communes with workforce in the chemical sectors seemed to favor the three projects, although the effect is significant for the solar initiative only
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Omitted sectoral exposure variable directly tied to referendum support.
Omitted sectoral exposure variable; signs vary across specifications and proposals.

missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: the transport sector variable has a positive impact on the voting outcome and is statistically significant at the one percent level for two of the three projects
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Omitted sectoral exposure variable; mixed signs across the full-model specifications.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper. Metadata are correct and the study is clearly tax-specific. The main issue is completeness: three sectoral exposure determinants are missing, and several extracted covariates are better labeled as controls.
